notes: Right album, unambiguous. albumDNA body is verbatim from the matching wiki album page (trumpeter; only string-orchestra album; recorded Oct 4-10 1999; released 2000 by Verve). MB (search, sim 1.0) confirms Verve, 2000-05-23; all 5 keyTracks are in the 12-track list. ours.label='Unknown' is a data gap (MB and prose both say Verve) - not a contradiction. The appended sentence 'won two Grammy Awards ... in 1998 and 2002' is not in the provided album wiki extract and cannot be confirmed from the pack; it is a peripheral biographical aside, not part of the album's checkable identity, so kept clean but flag for a repair-writer to confirm.

MB (embedded_mbid, authoritative) credits 'Roy Haynes Trio, Danilo Perez, John Patitucci', Verve, 2000 - matches ours.label/year and all 5 keyTracks. The actual leader is Roy Haynes; ours attributes the album to sideman Danilo Perez, who IS a credited artist on the release, so this is an imprecise leader attribution, not a contradiction. The Norman Granz/Verve/Ella-to-Oscar-Peterson line is generic label color (editorial), not a per-album claim. No wiki stored.

MB (embedded_mbid) and the matching wiki page agree: Lee Konitz / Don Friedman / Attila Zoller, recorded Switzerland 1995, released on the Swiss hat label; ours.label=hatOLOGY and year=2000 match this MB release (a later hatOLOGY issue). albumDNA body is verbatim from the wiki page. All 5 keyTracks present in MB. The 'free improvisation output from 2000' clause is editorial filler.

CONTENT-QUALITY ISSUE, not a disambiguation collision. MB (embedded_mbid) confirms the album: title '[Vento]', credited 'Barbara Casini, Enrico Rava', Label Bleu, 2000 - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks are in the MB list. But albumDNA contains ZERO album-specific content: it is a generic Enrico Rava bio ('Italian jazz trumpeter ... started on trombone, then changed to the trumpet after hearing Miles Davis'), copied verbatim from the wiki ARTIST page. Those bio facts are true and supported by that artist page, so there is no false claim. wikipediaUrl ships the Enrico Rava artist page, not an album page, hence wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity (it IS the correct artist, just the wrong page type). Repair-writer should replace the bio with actual album prose and fix/remove the link. Note the album is a Casini co-credit (Rava is the listed artist).

MB (embedded_mbid) credits Louis Armstrong, G.H.B. Records, 2001 - ours.artist/label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. wikipediaUrl ships the generic 'Jazz' genre page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jazz), which is NOT about this album -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity; that page is unusable for fact-checking. The substantive claim 'recorded during the swing era' cannot be checked against any pack evidence (MB gives no recording dates) and is suspect: this is a 2001 G.H.B. trad-jazz release whose MB tracklist includes 'Wonderful World' (a 1967 song) and 'I Dig Satch', suggesting later/tribute/compilation material rather than swing-era Armstrong recordings. Not flagging as factual_error because no evidence passage contradicts it. Repair-writer must verify what this release actually is and when it was recorded.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Lars Jansson, Imogena AB, 2001 (SE) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks are in the MB list. No URL was stored (wikiLinkVerdict=none_stored); the opensearch fallback landed on a 'Ballad (disambiguation)' page that is unrelated to this album and is discarded for fact-checking. 'jazz fusion recording' is a genre characterization (editorial), not a checkable claim. No contradictions.

Fully corroborated. MB (embedded_mbid): Jason Moran, Blue Note, 2001-08. Matching wiki album page; albumDNA body is verbatim ('third album led by Jason Moran ... regular trio alongside veteran saxophonist Sam Rivers, who plays tenor, soprano, flute and piano'). All 5 keyTracks appear in both MB and the wiki track listing.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Willie 'The Lion' Smith, Gitanes Jazz Productions, 2001 (FR) - ours.artist/label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. No wiki stored. The 'contemporary jazz tradition' framing is stylistically off (Willie the Lion Smith was a stride pianist who died in 1973; this is the archival 'Jazz in Paris' reissue series), but that is an editorial genre tag, not a checkable factual claim, and nothing in the pack contradicts it. No contradictions.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Jim Hall, Telarc Jazz, 2001-09-25 - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. The album's defining premise - duets/trios with named bassists Dave Holland, Christian McBride, Charlie Haden, George Mraz and Scott Colley - is a substantive personnel claim that cannot be checked: MB carries no personnel and no wiki was available. No contradiction either; flagged unverifiable so a repair-writer confirms the bassist line-up.

MB (embedded_mbid) credits 'Vinnie Colaiuta, Robben Ford, Jimmy Haslip', no label, 2001 - all 5 keyTracks present; ours.label='Unknown' is consistent with MB having no label. The bio sentence 'Colaiuta has won one Grammy Award and has been nominated twice' is verbatim from, and confirmed by, the provided Vinnie Colaiuta wiki ARTIST page. wikipediaUrl ships that artist page (correct artist, drummer) rather than an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. No false claims. Note the album is a Jing Chi trio (Colaiuta/Ford/Haslip); ours lists only Colaiuta.

MB (search, sim 1.0) confirms Roy Eldridge, Original Jazz Classics, 2001 - all 5 keyTracks present. albumDNA is the Roy Eldridge wiki ARTIST bio verbatim (nicknamed 'Little Jazz', American jazz trumpeter, tritone substitutions, impact on Dizzy Gillespie, swing era / precursor of bebop) plus an editorial OJC closing line; all bio facts are supported by the provided artist page. wikipediaUrl ships that artist page (correct artist) not an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. No false claims; albumDNA is a misplaced artist bio rather than album prose - flag for repair-writer.
MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Kenny Burrell, Concord Jazz, 2001 - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. Remaining prose is editorial era/genre boilerplate. No wiki; no contradictions.

Original-vs-reissue split, both internally consistent. The catalogued release is a 2001 Past Perfect Silver Line compilation (MB embedded_mbid, releaseGroup secondaryType 'Compilation', DE); ours.label='Past Perfect Silver Line' and year=2001 match MB, and all 5 keyTracks (Hornin' In, Let's Cool One, Little Rootie Tootie, Monk's Dream, Bemsha Swing) are in this MB release's tracklist. albumDNA, however, is verbatim from the Monk's Music wiki page describing the ORIGINAL 1957 Riverside album (Thelonious Monk Septet with Coleman Hawkins and John Coltrane; released Nov 1957 on Riverside; recorded NYC June 26 1957) - that famous album has a different program (Abide with Me / Well You Needn't / Ruby My Dear / Off Minor / Epistrophy / Crepuscule with Nellie). No contradiction to flag: the prose facts are true of the original, the fields/keyTracks are true of the Past Perfect reissue, and the wiki link is to the right work. Note for repair-writer: the prose describes the original Riverside LP while this catalog row is a 2001 Past Perfect compilation with a different tracklist; the 'Riverside Records' mention in prose is the original label, not this release's label.

Fully corroborated. MB (embedded_mbid): Shirley Horn, Verve, 2001-03-13. Matching wiki album page; albumDNA body verbatim ('2001 studio album by Shirley Horn, arranged by Johnny Mandel'). All 5 keyTracks appear in both MB and the wiki track listing. Verve/Granz closing line is editorial label color.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Nils Petter Molvaer, EmArcy, 2002 - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present; the provided wiki artist page's discography also lists 'NP3 - EmArcy (2002)'. albumDNA is the Molvaer wiki ARTIST bio verbatim ('also known as NPM ... Norwegian jazz trumpeter ... pioneer of future jazz ... best showcased on Khmer'); all bio facts supported by that page. wikipediaUrl ships the artist page (correct artist) not an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. No false claims; albumDNA is a misplaced artist bio - flag for repair-writer.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Clark Terry, Chesky Records - all 5 keyTracks present. ours.year=2002 matches MB releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 2002 (the specific release shows 2008, i.e. a reissue/edition). No URL stored (none_stored); the opensearch fallback hit a 'One on One' disambiguation page, unrelated, discarded. Remaining prose is editorial. No contradictions.

MB (embedded_mbid) credits 'Enrico Pieranunzi, Marc Johnson, Joey Baron', CAM Jazz, 2002-03-25 (IT) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present; the provided Pieranunzi wiki artist page lists 'Play Morricone (CAM Jazz, 2002)' and confirms he is an Italian jazz pianist. wikipediaUrl ships the artist page, not an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. albumDNA does describe the album but is padded with duplicated template filler. No false claims.

Fully corroborated. MB (embedded_mbid): Julius Hemphill, Sackville Recordings, 2002 (CA). Matching wiki album page; albumDNA body verbatim (solo 'audiodrama', recorded Larchmont NY Nov 1976 + Toronto Mar 1977, released on vinyl by Sackville 1977 as a 2-LP set, reissued on CD 2001, Hemphill plays alto/soprano sax and flute via overdubbing). All 5 listed keyTracks (movements I-V) are in the MB tracklist (MB has I-VII).

MB (search, sim 1.0) credits 'Marc Copland, Greg Osby', Nagel Heyer Records, 2002 (DE) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. ours attributes the album to Greg Osby, who is a co-credited artist (actual co-leader Marc Copland). The 'alto saxophonist' and 'M-Base recordings' references are supported by the provided Greg Osby wiki ARTIST page; the 'reflective/mature/searching' language is editorial opinion. wikipediaUrl ships the Osby artist page, not an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. No false claims. Note co-leader Marc Copland is omitted.

MB (search, sim 0.75) confirms 'Tomasz Stanko Quartet', ECM Records, 2002-01-06 (DE) - ours.label=ECM/year=2002 match and all 6 listed keyTracks (Soul of Things I-VI) are in the MB tracklist (MB has I-XIII). No wiki available. Multiple substantive factual claims cannot be checked against the pack: the backing trio being Marcin Wasilewski's, the link to Krzysztof Komeda's film scores, and 'one of ECM's best-selling albums of the 2000s' / 'introduced Stanko and the Wasilewski trio to international audiences'. MB's 'Quartet' credit is consistent with Stanko-plus-trio but does not name the personnel. No contradiction; flagged unverifiable for a repair-writer to confirm personnel and the sales/impact claims. Much of the rest ('haunting', 'dark fragile tone', 'Eastern European melancholy') is editorial opinion.

MB (search, sim 1.0) confirms Art Tatum, High Definition Jazz, 2002 (GB) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. This is a 2002 reissue/compilation of older Tatum recordings (Tatum died 1956), consistent with MB. The closing sentence ('extended jazz piano's vocabulary ... reharmonization, voicing, and bitonality') is verbatim from, and confirmed by, the provided Art Tatum wiki ARTIST page. wikipediaUrl ships that artist page (correct artist) not an album page -> wikiLinkVerdict=wrong_entity. No false claims.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms artist 'Jay Dee' (= J Dilla), Bling47 Recordings, 2002 (US) - ours.artist/label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. No wiki. The 'contemporary jazz date' genre tag is a stretch (J Dilla is a hip-hop producer and this is an instrumental beat compilation), but genre framing is editorial, not a checkable factual claim, and nothing in the pack contradicts it. No contradictions.

MB (embedded_mbid) confirms Scott Henderson, Shrapnel Records, 2002 (US) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present; MB's track 10 'Rituals' supports the prose's mention of a 'Rituals' track. The substantive claims 'his third solo album' and that 'Rituals' is a re-recording of a Tribal Tech song cannot be checked against the pack (no wiki; MB gives no such context). No contradiction; flagged unverifiable for a repair-writer to confirm. 'returning to his blues-roots' is editorial.

Fully corroborated. MB (embedded_mbid): Wayne Shorter, Verve, 2003-03-25. Matching wiki album page; albumDNA body verbatim ('studio album by saxophonist Wayne Shorter, released on Verve Records in 2003 ... second album to feature the Footprints Quartet of Shorter, Danilo Perez, John Patitucci and Brian Blade'). All 5 keyTracks appear in both MB and the wiki track listing.

Fully corroborated. MB (embedded_mbid): 'Antipop Consortium, Matthew Shipp', Thirsty Ear, 2003-02-18 (US) - ours.label/year match and all 5 keyTracks present. Matching wiki album page (titled 'Antipop vs. Matthew Shipp'); albumDNA body verbatim ('collaborative studio album by Antipop Consortium and Matthew Shipp ... released on Thirsty Ear Recordings on February 18, 2003'). Closing line is editorial.
